prompt:
aliassync.ps1 keeps the host-alias directory on the relay box in line with the desired-state JSON from git: it prints a create/update/remove plan and applies it. Works fine, one problem: during change freezes the on-call still wants to SEE the plan, and right now the only way to see it is to run the thing, which applies it. People have been copying the directory aside first, which tells you everything about how much they trust the tool.

Feature request, straight from the freeze retro: make it a proper ShouldProcess citizen.

- Declare `SupportsShouldProcess` and gate EVERY file mutation -- each create, each update, each remove -- through `$PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess`, one call per file, with the alias file name (`<name>.alias`) as the target. Not one blanket gate around the whole apply loop; the point is that the What-if chatter reads as an auditable per-file list.
- `-WhatIf`: the plan prints exactly as it does today (the harness strips pwsh's own 'What if:' lines before comparing), the directory is byte-for-byte untouched afterwards, exit 0.
- `-Confirm:$false`: applies non-interactively, same output and same end state as a plain run.
- A plain run keeps doing exactly what it does today: plan, then apply, exit 0. All current behavior stays frozen -- section order create/update/remove with ordinal names inside each, `.alias` files only (foreign files never touched), targets written as `<target>\n`, the no-op silent run, and the 65/66 error contract with nothing on stdout and nothing modified.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_aliassync.ps1` from the workspace root, unmodified. Do not touch the test file.
notes: 6P7 item 4: reconciler grows -WhatIf/-Confirm via SupportsShouldProcess, on a 6P4-#6-SHAPED tool (desired-state JSON vs on-disk directory -> ordered create/update/remove plan) with FRESH files -- no dependency on ps-svcplan. files/ ships the WORKING reconciler (19 existing-behavior checks green at baseline); verify fails only because -WhatIf/-Confirm are unknown parameters. PowerShell-native surface (no bash/py analog, catalog's stated point): [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess)] on a script, per-mutation $PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess gating pinned behaviorally -- harness counts exactly 4 'What if:' lines (one per planned mutation, so a blanket gate fails) and requires each gate to name its .alias target; 'What if:' chatter lands on child stdout (probe-verified 2026-07-16) and is stripped before the byte-exact plan compare; literal '-Confirm:$false' binding through pwsh -File probe-verified. Directory frozen under -WhatIf proven by full name=content snapshot compare. Differentiation vs ps-rotaplan (6P4 #5, ships WITH ShouldProcess as a build spec): here ShouldProcess is the FEATURE grown onto working code under existing-behavior pins, and the domain is desired-state file reconciliation, not age-based log rotation.
